summary: MESSES is a Python package that facilitates the conversion of tabular data into other formats.
The convert command transforms JSON format to the JSON version of supported formats and then to the final niche format.
The convert command supports simple JSON-to-JSON conversion through the "generic" sub-command.
The extract command supports turning tabular data into JSON by adding a layer of tags on top of the data.
The override field in conversion directives allows direct specification of a string value.
The code field in conversion directives allows Python code to be delivered directly to eval().
The for_each field in str directives allows looping over all records in a table.
The delimiter field in str directives specifies how to separate strings built from each record.
The test field in str directives filters records based on field equality conditions.
The sort_by field in str directives allows sorting input JSON records before building the value.
The matrix directive creates a list of dictionaries by looping over all records in the indicated table.
The collate field in matrix directives groups record data into dictionaries based on a specified field value.
The fields_to_headers field changes matrix directive behavior to copy all fields from input records as is into the dictionary.
The exclusion_headers field in matrix directives allows excluding specific fields from being added.
The values_to_str field in matrix directives can convert all field values to strings.
The required field in conversion directives indicates if a directive is required, stopping the program on error if true.
The default field in conversion directives provides a string value to default to if the directive cannot be built and is not required.
